name: [Meal Prep] X Content Poster

nodes.name: Find oldest approved + post to X

main.node: Find oldest approved + post to X

_setup_notes: APPROVAL IS NOT HANDLED BY THIS WORKFLOW ON PURPOSE. Only one workflow can own your bot's Telegram webhook (the existing Inbox workflow) -- adding a second Telegram Trigger here would silently steal it. Instead: approving a draft means telling Claude directly (in chat) to flip content/queue/<id>.md's status from draft to approved and push -- Claude does the git commit, this workflow just watches for status: approved on its own schedule. No new Telegram wiring needed.

Create a new n8n credential of type 'Twitter OAuth1 API' named exactly 'X API - Meal Prep' (matching this app's existing app-suffixed naming convention, D-049) using the Consumer Key, Consumer Secret, Access Token, and Access Token Secret from developer.x.com's Keys and Tokens page -- the OAuth 1.0 section, NOT OAuth 2.0. Confirm the app's 'User authentication settings' are set to Read and Write BEFORE generating the Access Token, or posting will fail with a permissions error.

The core logic lives in ONE Code node ('Find oldest approved + post to X') rather than spread across several visual nodes, on purpose: posting a thread is an inherently SEQUENTIAL, dependent operation (tweet 2 needs tweet 1's returned id, tweet 3 needs tweet 2's, etc.), which n8n's standard loop nodes (Split In Batches) aren't built for -- they're designed for independent per-item work. A single script is also easier to hand back to Claude to debug later than a multi-node loop's wiring.

THIS NEEDS A LIVE TEST BEFORE YOU TRUST THE SCHEDULE. Specifically the Code node's use of `this.helpers.httpRequestWithAuthentication` with two different attached credentials on one node -- this is standard n8n Code node behavior in recent versions, but hasn't been run against your actual instance. Test by: put one queue file at status: approved, then manually execute this workflow once (the 'Execute workflow' button in the n8n editor) and watch it post for real -- not a dry run, it will actually post to X. Check the result before trusting the every-2-days schedule.

If your n8n version doesn't support attaching two credential types to one Code node, split 'Find oldest approved + post to X' into two Code nodes -- one with only httpHeaderAuth attached (GitHub read + filter, outputs the chosen file's parsed tweets), one with only twitterOAuth1Api attached (does just the posting loop). Ask Claude to make this split if needed; the logic doesn't otherwise change.

Set this workflow's Error Workflow (3-dot menu -> Settings -> Error Workflow) to '[Meal Prep] Error Alert -> Telegram', same as the other three workflows (D-050) -- so a failed post (e.g. an expired X token) reaches Telegram instead of failing silently. This is exactly the same class of silent-failure risk logged in docs/CONTENT_LOG.md's own outage story -- worth not repeating here.

Cadence is set to every 2 days at 10:00 UTC (cron: 0 10 */2 * *) -- edit the Schedule Trigger node's expression directly in the n8n UI to change it. If nothing is status: approved when it fires, the workflow just ends quietly (not an error) -- see the 'Posted one?' node's false branch.
REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_TELEGRAM_USER_ID in the notify node -- same numeric id already used in your other three workflows.

Known limitation: a partial-thread failure (posts 3 of 9 tweets, then the 4th call fails) leaves those 3 live on X and the queue file untouched at status: approved, so the next scheduled run reposts the whole thread from tweet 1 -- rare, but if the error alert ever fires mid-thread, check the account and manually delete the orphaned partial thread before the next run.
